aboutFirst:
I'm a {age} year-old PhD student at the Trier University who is fascinated by both software and hardware.
My main goal is to earn my doctoral degree and contribute in my field.
aboutSecond: In my free time and besides programming, I do some workouts and currently learning 3D printing and modelling using CAD software. I also enjoy playing video games.
toolkitSubtitle: Tools I use to bring ideas to life.
toolJava: At the beginning of my studies in 2019 I began to learn the programming language Java. Throughout the years I learned to love this programming language. I like the class-based and object-oriented implementation.
toolPython: With Python I made my first steps in the programming world. In 2016/2017 I learned the basic concepts of coding with Python. It is not my favorite language because it is not a strictly typed language.
toolCSharp: C# is the first programming language which I learned on my own through a book. The reason why I chose this language is because I wanted to program video games and C# is similar to Java.
toolUnity: Back in 2021, I started to learn the Unity Engine through a book with a lot of tutorials. Today, I use it for creating interactive AR experiences that combine logic and creativity.
toolMetaSDK: As part of my master thesis, I began to work with the Meta SDK for the Meta Quest 3 headset. Now, I am using it to create AR applications for my own research projects.
toolDocker: For my own research, I use Docker to distribute application in containers, so that everyone has an easy access to them.
toolGit: For version control and tracking changes in my projects, I use Git and GitHub.
toolIntelliJ: For programming in Java, I use IntelliJ IDEA. It was the first IDE I used.
toolPyCharm: During the course of my studies, I began to use PyCharm for Python programming.
toolRider: For the development of AR applications in Unity, I use Rider.
toolVSCode: I am using Visual Studio Code for editing other files like Markdown or LaTeX files.
toolNotion: I use Notion alongside handwritten notes to capture and organize all relevant information and ideas. They help me to stay well-organized.
toolFreeCAD: I use the open-source software FreeCAD for 3D modeling. With sketches, I can create simple models and then export them for 3D printing.
toolBambuStudio: As an owner of a Bambu Lab 3D printer, I use Bambu Studio to print 3D models from MakerWorld and my own models.
timelinePhDDescription: Currently, I am pursuing a PhD in computer science, focusing my research on software engineering and augmented reality to develop advanced methods for designing interactive and immersive systems.
timelineMScTitle: Master of Science in Computer Science
timelineMScDescription: I finished my M.Sc. in computer science. During my master’s studies, I specialized in software engineering, focusing on advanced concepts of software development and program analysis.
timelineEcoDiveTitle: EcoDive: Enhancing Presence and Ambient Environmental Awareness in a Virtual Reality Experience for Underwater Marine Debris Collection
timelineEcoDiveDescription: As part of the Master’s module "VR and 3D Interactions", we created a demo for the VRST conference held in Trier, Germany. The project aims to highlight the global risks and environmental dangers associated with water pollution. The demo was developed in Unity and implemented for the Meta Quest 3 VR headset.
timelineEcoDiveImageAlt: Screenshot of demo: Seeing the difference between the clean and polluted ocean under water.
timelineBScDescription: finished my B.Sc. in computer science. During my bachelor’s studies, I developed a broad foundation in computer science, covering programming, software development, and algorithms. This enabled me to analyze complex problems and create practical software solutions.
timelinePortfolioWebsiteDescription: This website serves as a platform that showcases the diverse range of tools I work with, but also highlights the projects I have created.
timelineCreARtionDescription: CreARtion is an Android App which has been officially published on the Google PlayStore. In this App you can transform objects into sculptures in Augmented Reality. It was developed as part of a module called "Kleines Studienprojekt" at University Trier.
timelineCreARtionImageAlt: Screenshot of the CreARtion App: Manipulating a virtual sphere in AR.
timelineAbiturDescription: Achieved the general qualification for university entrance.
socialMediaSubtitle: Let us connect on social media!
